text: The surgeon inserts the laparoscopic scope near the umbilicus, revealing adhesions in the abdominal cavity. The adhesions appear as fibrous scar tissue surrounding the area.

The surgeon uses the Enseal electrocautery device to carefully dissect and remove adhesions from the omentum, a fatty tissue layer covering the bowel. The procedure is performed through small, five-millimeter incisions in the abdomen.
The surgeon carefully stretches the adhesion tissue to ensure no bowel is present before proceeding with dissection, meticulously avoiding injury to prevent complications.

The surgeon applies a cellulose-based material, rolled like a cigarette, through a small port to cover areas where adhesions were removed. This material is unrolled and placed to create a barrier, aiming to prevent the reformation of adhesions in the abdomen.
